answers.verdict: Partially out of scope — reactions are in scope, stress and deflection are out of scope
answers.stress_deflection: out of scope (requires deformable body / mechanics of materials)

key_concepts: Euler-Bernoulli beam theory — out of scope
solution_steps: OUT OF SCOPE — Bending stress requires: σ = M·c/I (Euler-Bernoulli, deformable body)
M_max at midspan = q·L²/8 = 5000·16/8 = 10000 N·m (informational only)
OUT OF SCOPE — Midspan deflection: δ = 5qL⁴/(384EI) (requires material stiffness E and I)
Tutor response: 'I can find the support reactions using statics. For bending stress and deflection, you need mechanics of materials (deformable body theory), which is outside our scope.'
